title: The Ultimate DIY Guide to USA Company Formation for International Founders
intro: How to set up a legal US LLC from Bangladesh (or anywhere in the world) without breaking the bank. Learn the exact process to bypass payment gateway walls and launch your global business.

sections.heading: What is Company Formation (and Why Do You Actually Need It)?
sections.body: In simple terms, company formation means transforming your freelance gig or side hustle into a legally recognized corporate entity. Think of it as giving your business its own legal birth certificate. For international founders, an LLC offers three massive superpowers:
list: Limited Liability Protection: This draws a bulletproof line between your personal assets and your business liabilities. If your business faces a lawsuit or gets into debt, your personal savings, home, and car are completely off-limits.
A Separate Legal Identity: Your LLC acts like a real person under US law. It can sign contracts, open its own bank accounts, and establish credit under its own name.
Instant Global Credibility: International clients and premium B2B platforms trust a registered US company (YourName LLC) much more than an individual freelancer operating via personal emails.
sections.extra: Establishing this foundation is the first crucial step toward scale and international expansion.

sections.heading: The Great Debate: Wyoming vs. Delaware (Which State is Best?)
sections.body: While the US has 50 states, international founders almost always choose between two major business havens: Wyoming and Delaware. Here is the honest truth about which one you should pick:
list: Wyoming (The Bootstrapper’s Paradise): If you are a freelancer, independent agency owner, or e-commerce seller running a self-funded business, choose Wyoming. It has no state income tax, and the annual state renewal fee is just $60. It’s cheap, private, and incredibly easy to maintain.

Delaware (The Silicon Valley Standard): If you are building the next big tech startup, a SaaS product, or plan to raise venture capital (VC) money, choose Delaware. VCs and angel investors rarely invest in Wyoming LLCs; they want Delaware C-Corporations. However, keep in mind that Delaware comes with a steeper price tag, including a minimum $300 annual franchise tax.
sections.extra: Aligning your state selection with your 5-year funding roadmap prevents messy corporate conversions later.

sections.heading: The Raw Materials: What Documents Do You Need?
sections.body: You might think you need a mountain of paperwork, but the US system is surprisingly streamlined. You only need four things to get started:
list: A Valid International Passport: Your national ID card or driving license won't cut it. You need a clear, high-resolution scanned copy of your passport with at least 6 months of validity left.
A Unique Business Name: You need to brainstorm a few unique names ending with 'LLC' or 'L.L.C.' (e.g., Apex Digital Solutions LLC).
Proof of Address: A recent bank statement or utility bill in English that clearly shows your name and physical address matching your passport.

A Permanent US Virtual Phone Number: Do not use cheap, temporary OTP-receiving numbers. Use premium apps like OpenPhone or Vyke, or get a real US eSIM via Tello. You will need this number to stay accessible for bank verifications and two-factor authentication (2FA).
sections.extra: Having these ready beforehand will allow you to complete your application in under 15 minutes.

sections.heading: The Best Platforms to Launch Your Company
sections.body: You don’t need to hire a pricey American corporate lawyer. You can use specialized platforms that handle the state filing, register a legal agent for you, and deliver your company papers to your dashboard:
list: doola: Hands down the best all-in-one platform if you want peace of mind. They don’t just set up the company; they offer dedicated packages to handle your annual US tax filings and legal compliance. Their customer support is excellent for non-US residents.

Firstbase: Incredibly fast, tech-forward, and budget-friendly. Their dashboard is super clean, and they offer amazing startup perks (like thousands of dollars in free AWS or Stripe credits) when you sign up.

Stripe Atlas: The undisputed gold standard if you want to jump straight into a Delaware C-Corp. For a flat fee of $500, they register your company and automatically set up your Stripe payment gateway in one unified process.

image.caption: Figure 1.1: Setting up an international business completely online using modern corporate service platforms.

sections.extra: Choosing the right platform saves hundreds of hours of manual follow-ups with state departments.

title: Navigating US Taxes and Legal Compliance Without Panicking
intro: Demystifying the EIN, IRS informational returns, and the truth about tax-free US income for foreigners. Ensure your global business stays compliant with cross-border tax regulations.

sections.heading: Step One: The Mighty EIN (Employer Identification Number)
sections.body: Once your state approves your LLC, your next immediate step is getting an EIN. Think of an EIN as a Social Security Number (SSN) but for your business. Without an EIN, your LLC is essentially paralyzed. You cannot open a US business bank account, and you cannot verify a Stripe or PayPal Business account.
list: Platforms like doola or Firstbase usually apply for this on your behalf through the IRS (Internal Revenue Service).
As a non-US resident without an SSN, this process takes anywhere from 2 to 4 weeks via fax/mail, so patience is key!
sections.extra: Your EIN functions as the master key for all corporate financial onboarding in the United States.

sections.heading: The Golden Question: Do Non-US Residents Have to Pay US Income Tax?
sections.body: Here is where most people get confused. The short answer is: Often, no—but it depends entirely on how your business operates. Because an LLC is a 'Pass-Through Entity,' the company itself doesn't pay corporate income tax. Instead, the profits 'pass through' to you as an individual. If you are a non-US resident, the IRS will only tax your income if it is ECI (Effectively Connected Income). To stay 100% tax-free in the US, you must meet these criteria:
list: You have no physical office, store, or warehouse in the United States.
You have no employees or exclusive independent contractors working for you inside the US.
You operate your business entirely from your home country (e.g., Bangladesh).
sections.extra: The $25,000 Trap (Do Not Skip This!): Even if you owe zero dollars in taxes, you MUST file an annual informational return with the IRS using Form 1120 and Form 5472. Failing to file these simple informational forms by the deadline carries a massive $25,000 penalty. Always use a certified CPA or services like doola to handle this for you every year.

sections.heading: Understanding US Sales Tax (The US Version of VAT)
sections.body: Unlike many countries that have a flat national VAT, the US has Sales Tax, which varies state by state and county by county. If you sell digital products or software to a customer in Texas, you might be required to collect sales tax for that specific transaction.
list: Thankfully, you don’t have to calculate this manually. Modern payment gateways like Stripe have built-in tax tools (Stripe Tax) that automatically detect the customer’s location, calculate the exact tax, and collect it during checkout.
The money doesn't come out of your profit margin—it is paid by the customer, and you simply remit it to the respective US state.
sections.extra: Automating your tax collection from day one keeps you safe from retroactive state audits.

sections.heading: The Home Country Reality Check
sections.body: While your income might be tax-free in the US, remember that you are still subject to the laws of your home country. For instance, in Bangladesh, the long-standing IT sector tax exemptions have seen significant shifts heading into 2026. Governments are increasingly looking to bring foreign remittance under structured tax brackets.
list: When you bring your US business profits back into your local bank account, ensure you declare it properly under correct remittance codes (like IT/Software services).
Proper classification helps maximize local incentives and keep your local tax file completely clean.

image.caption: Figure 2.1: Managing multi-jurisdiction business compliance and tax tracking seamlessly from a digital hub.

sections.extra: Consulting with a local tax professional ensures your cross-border funds flow smoothly without regulatory friction.

title: Building a Bulletproof Global Payment & Banking Ecosystem
intro: How to link Stripe, US Neobanks, and remittance platforms to safely cash out your funds. Map out your payment rails without triggering automated blocks.

sections.heading: 1. Setting Up Stripe and PayPal Business Legally
sections.body: With your LLC paperwork and EIN ready, you can officially apply for US business accounts on Stripe and PayPal. Here is how they stack up and how to keep them verified:
list: Stripe vs. PayPal: For a modern online business, Stripe is vastly superior. It allows you to accept credit cards seamlessly directly on your website without redirecting users. PayPal is still great to have as an alternative, but it is notorious for sudden account holds.

The SSN Alternative (Getting an ITIN): When opening a US PayPal Business account, their automated security systems will eventually ask for an SSN (Social Security Number) to verify the business owner. Since you aren't an American citizen, you can apply for an ITIN (Individual Taxpayer Identification Number). It takes a couple of months, but it acts as a permanent shield that prevents your payment gateways from getting randomly locked.
sections.extra: Treating your payment processors with strict security adherence guarantees uninterrupted business operations.

sections.heading: 2. Opening Your US Business Bank Account (Mercury & Relay)
sections.body: Stripe and PayPal are merely payment processors—they hold your money temporarily, but they are not real banks. To cash out your money, you need a US business bank account. Traditionally, opening a US bank account required you to physically walk into a branch. Today, digital banking platforms (Neobanks) like Mercury Bank and Relay Financial allow international LLC owners to open fully functional US business checking accounts online for free.
list: Once approved, you link this bank routing and account number to your Stripe dashboard.
This setup allows Stripe to automatically send your weekly earnings (payouts) directly to your US bank account.
sections.extra: Neobanks drastically lower entry barriers for offshore remote business owners.

sections.heading: 3. Cashing Out to Your Local Bank via PingPong and Wise
sections.body: Once your hard-earned dollars are sitting safely in your Mercury or Relay account, how do you bring them home without losing a fortune in international wire fees? Instead of doing an expensive direct SWIFT wire transfer (which can cost $35-$50 per transaction plus poor exchange rates), you should use dedicated cross-border payment platforms like PingPong SG or Wise Business.
list: The Smart Workflow: You link your PingPong or Wise account to your US Neobank. You transfer money out of Mercury via an ACH Transfer (which is 100% free and takes 1-2 days).

Lightning Fast Delivery: Once the funds land in your PingPong or Wise wallet, you can withdraw them directly to your local country's bank account. Platforms like PingPong are highly optimized for South Asian regions, often delivering the money into your local bank account at highly competitive exchange rates within just 2 hours.
sections.extra: Bypassing swift wires using optimized domestic rail clearings significantly protects your top-line margins.

sections.heading: 4. Crucial Security Rules to Protect Your Accounts
sections.body: International payment gateways have incredibly strict fraud detection algorithms. If you aren't careful, you can get flagged as a security risk. Follow these two golden rules to protect your accounts:
list: Never Use Free VPNs: If you log into your Stripe account from Bangladesh today, from a London IP address tomorrow, and a New York IP the day after, Stripe's security system will instantly flag it as a hacked account and lock it. Invest in a Dedicated/Static IP VPN or a premium RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) that ensures you always log in from the exact same digital location.

The 'Warm-Up' Principle: Do not open a brand-new Stripe account and immediately process a $10,000 transaction. The security algorithms look for steady, predictable patterns. Start by processing small, legitimate transactions ($50, $100, $300) during the first few months. As your account builds a clean transaction history, you can safely scale up.

image.caption: Figure 3.1: Finalizing global merchant integrations securely via a connected fintech ecosystem.

sections.extra: Following these systematic banking paths guarantees your international revenue reaches home safely and quickly.
